(4) Gate launch on a minimum content threshold per venue page (photos, description, courts, pricing) to avoid thin-content; consider noindex on venues with status != active. (5) Add hreflang/x-default plan now even though EN is later, since URL structure is hard to change post-launch.

findings.area: Self-hosted fonts + critical CSS + LCP image discipline (under-specified mechanics)
findings.issue: Section 8.1 says the right words (woff2 subset, font-display: swap, preload, inline critical CSS, preload LCP image) but gives no mechanism, and these are precisely where teams lose the last 2-3 Lighthouse points. font-display: swap causes a visible FOUT/layout shift unless the fallback font metrics are matched (size-adjust/ascent-override) — uncontrolled, this hurts CLS and the perceived-stability score. 'Inline critical CSS' with Tailwind requires a per-route critical-CSS extraction step that the Vite/Tailwind pipeline does NOT do out of the box. Preloading the 'LCP image' assumes a single known LCP element, but on a venue page the LCP is often the hero photo whose URL is dynamic per venue — a static preload tag won't help and a wrong preload wastes bandwidth. AVIF encoding for user-uploaded venue photos needs a server-side image pipeline (not mentioned).
findings.recommendation: (1) Specify a font strategy: self-host woff2, subset to Latin (+ Indonesian glyphs), preload ONLY the 1-2 critical weights, and add size-adjust/ascent-override metric overrides for the fallback to kill the swap CLS. (2) Pick a concrete critical-CSS approach (e.g. a build-time critical extraction per template, or accept Tailwind's already-small purged CSS inlined wholesale if it's <14KB after purge — measure it). (3) Make the LCP-image preload DYNAMIC: emit in the SSR head with the actual per-venue hero URL + fetchpriority=high on the ; never a hardcoded path. (4) Specify the image pipeline: on upload, generate responsive AVIF+WebP derivatives (width/height known => no CLS) via a queue job; the spec mentions WebP/AVIF output but not who produces them. (5) Add these as explicit Lighthouse CI assertions (CLS < 0.1, unused-CSS, properly-sized-images).

findings.area: Lighthouse 98 across ALL categories including PWA/Best-Practices/SEO on the AUTHENTICATED dashboard
findings.issue: The goal says '>=98 all categories mobile'. Two gotchas: (1) The venue dashboard is a heavily-interactive Livewire app (calendar, bookings grid, pricing editor) — interactive admin UIs almost never hit 98 Performance on emulated mobile because of large DOM, table rendering, and Livewire round-trips; if the gate is applied here it will block CI perpetually. (2) Lighthouse's PWA category was deprecated/removed in recent Lighthouse versions, so 'all categories' is ambiguous — the installability and SW checks moved out of a scored category. Best-Practices can silently drop below 98 from things outside the team's control (e.g. third-party cookie warnings, console errors from Livewire in some states, missing CSP/HSTS headers). The spec doesn't define WHICH Lighthouse version/config the CI gate uses, so the target is unpinned and will drift.
findings.recommendation: (1) Restrict the >=98 CI gate to public, unauthenticated, SSR routes only; for the dashboard, track perf as a non-blocking trend with a looser budget, not a merge gate. (2) Pin the Lighthouse CI version and config in the repo (lighthouserc) and define exactly which categories are gated (Perf/Best-Practices/SEO/A11y) and what the installability assertion is now that PWA isn't a scored category — assert manifest + SW + offline fallback via explicit audits instead of a 'PWA score'. (3) Add CSP/HSTS/security headers to the spec (also needed for Best-Practices and the payment webhook surface) so Best-Practices doesn't silently fail. (4) Capture a baseline run early; don't discover at the end that an unpinned Lighthouse upgrade moved the goalposts.

synth.verdict: VenuePro v1 is architecturally coherent and names the right hard problems, but it is simultaneously over-scoped (five independently hard systems behind brutal non-functional gates) and dangerously under-specified on the load-bearing details: the double-booking guarantee, the money-custody/licensing model, and every operational policy a court business actually runs on. It is build-ready as a vision and as a booking-engine skeleton, but NOT build-ready as written — several P0 correctness, compliance, and economic gaps would cause money loss, legal exposure, or double-bookings on opening week. Re-baseline to a thin slice (locked booking + payment + dashboard + notifications, manual payout, VPR/Open Match deferred), pin the missing constraints and policies, and resolve the fund-custody model before any code.
synth.biggestRisk: Fund custody and the coupled double-booking guarantee. The money flow as written ("collect 100% → pool/aggregate venue funds nightly → gross−1% → disburse") makes VenuePro an unlicensed custodian of third-party funds (PJP/escrow exposure under BI 23/6/2021 + PBI 10/2025, and PMK 37/2025 0.5% PPh-collector regime), while the booking integrity rests only on a non-durable Redis lock + READ-COMMITTED re-check with no DB-level overlap constraint. Either one alone is launch-blocking; resolve custody first by adopting Xendit xenPlatform split-rules so the platform never holds venue money, and make the database the booking source of truth via a btree_gist EXCLUDE constraint — these two decisions ripple through the entire payout, refund, tax, and booking schema and must be settled before building.

findings.area: Fund custody / licensing — VenuePro must never hold venue funds
findings.fix: Make 'platform never custodies/aggregates venue money' an architectural invariant. Adopt Xendit xenPlatform per-transaction split rules: at charge time the venue's 99% lands in the venue's Xendit-held sub-account, platform receives only its 1% fee; Xendit (licensed PJP) is the legal fund-holder. Delete the 'nightly aggregate gross then subtract fee' pooling model — it is both a licensing risk and unnecessary float. Document the sub-account/split-rule account type in the spec and Xendit onboarding.

findings.area: Double-booking prevention — DB integrity, not just Redis
findings.fix: Make the database the source of truth. Add a tstzrange (generated from starts_at + duration_min) and a btree_gist EXCLUDE constraint on (court_id =, range &&) WHERE status IN ('hold','paid','confirmed') so overlap is a hard 23P01 error regardless of Redis failover/TTL-loss/READ-COMMITTED races. Keep Redis only as a fast-fail UX optimization. Route the walk-in/staff path through the SAME constraint and lock. Range overlap also correctly handles 90-min vs 60-min crossings that a discrete-slot UNIQUE would miss.

findings.area: Hold-expiry vs payment-window race + webhook idempotency/ordering
findings.fix: Set the Xendit charge expiry equal to (or just under) the 10-min hold and pass it explicitly. On 'paid' webhook do an atomic conditional transition (UPDATE ... WHERE id=? AND status='hold' AND hold_expires_at>now()); if 0 rows, treat as late payment → auto-refund + notify (define who eats the Xendit fee). Add webhook_events(provider, event_id UNIQUE) insert-first idempotency, verify x-callback-token, process inside a per-booking transaction with an explicit allowed-transition matrix so terminal states aren't overwritten by stale/out-of-order paid-then-failed events. Make all downstream effects (WA, payout) idempotent on payment_id.

findings.area: Cancellation / no-show / refund / venue-closure policy (entirely undefined)
findings.fix: Add a first-class per-venue CancellationPolicy (platform defaults): tiered refund % by time-to-start, explicit Xendit-fee bearer, venue-initiated cancellation = 100% auto-refund (+optional credit), configurable grace period, auto no-show marking (e.g. 15 min, staff-overridable), and weather/rain-out handling (critical for Jakarta outdoor courts). Specify refund mechanics (Xendit refund vs platform credit) per channel since QRIS/e-wallet refunds are slow/partial/sometimes unsupported, and a post-settlement clawback path. This is THE most operationally load-bearing policy and must precede any booking code.

findings.area: Court blocks / maintenance / holiday hour overrides (missing)
findings.fix: Add a court_blocks / availability_exceptions entity (court_id/venue_id, starts_at/ends_at, reason=maintenance|event|holiday|weather|private, recurring?) plus date-specific operating_hours overrides layered over the weekday defaults (Idul Fitri/Christmas/Independence Day). The Redis-locked + EXCLUDE-constrained availability engine must check blocks AND bookings before creating a hold. Surface block creation in the venue dashboard calendar. Without this, the platform can sell a court the owner closed for resurfacing — a guaranteed opening-week refund + reputation hit.

findings.area: Indonesia tax: PMK 37/2025 0.5% PPh collector + PPN on commission
findings.fix: The full-amount escrow-style flow is exactly the PMK 37/2025 target. Add a tax engine: capture each venue NPWP + turnover/PKP status; implement the 0.5% PPh withholding on gross-ex-VAT with the IDR 500m small-seller exemption (signed statements), monthly deposit/filing, and per-transaction bukti potong. Separately, model PPN correctly: the booking is the venue's sale (venue's PPN); VenuePro's taxable supply is its 1% commission, so register PKP and issue an e-faktur (Coretax) for 1%+PPN to the venue. Add pph_withheld, npwp, exemption_flag, ppn fields to payouts; decide whether 1% is PPN-inclusive (1.12% effective). Get a real tax position reviewed before finalizing payout schema.

findings.area: Unit economics + payout/settlement timing (1% is likely negative-margin)
findings.fix: Separate platform commission from Xendit MDR. State explicitly who absorbs MDR (QRIS ~0.7%, VA/e-wallet fees often >1%) — if the platform, gross margin is negative at 1%. Record the REAL per-payment provider fee from the webhook (don't estimate) and recompute viability (likely 3-5% or a flat per-booking fee for v1). Split booking confirmation (instant, webhook-driven) from money movement (T+2 Xendit settlement-driven): add settlement_status/settled_at, follow Xendit settlement cadence for payout, and aggregate payouts off an append-only ledger, not mutable bookings, so refunds/clawbacks reconcile. Walk-ins are inventory-only — exclude from fee/payout/withholding bases.

findings.area: Scope: re-baseline v1 to a thin slice; defer VPR + Open Match
findings.fix: v1 bundles five independently-hard systems (locked booking+payment, marketplace payout+tax, Glicko-2 rating, Open Match matchmaking, Livewire dashboard) behind hard gates — not shippable in 2 sprints. Cut v1 to: identity, venue/court, discovery, booking, payments + MANUAL payout, notifications, dashboard, PWA shell. Move VPR and Open Match to v1.5, automated payout+PPN disbursement to v1.5. Sequence in gates: G1 booking+payment proven with real money/no double-booking; G2 add 'friendly match with confirmed score' as the first rating source (breaks the rating↔matchmaking circular dependency); G3 ship Open Match on real ratings. Rename current doc 'product vision', write a separate sprint-sized build spec.

findings.area: Marketplace cold-start / two-sided liquidity (no plan)
findings.fix: Add an explicit supply-led go-to-market section: hand-onboard 3-8 concierge anchor venues in one Jakarta cluster BEFORE player acquisition; make off-peak Open Match (v1.5) the utilisation wedge; require an import path for a venue's existing forward bookings + a 'powered by your IG/WA' brand-deflection so venues don't lose their funnel. Define a written liquidity threshold (N active rated players, Y venues) and HIDE (not show empty) Open Match/VPR/radius-discovery below it. Validate willingness-to-accept the 1% + delayed-payout model with anchor venues before committing engineering.

findings.area: Row-level multi-tenancy: global-scope leakage & admin bypass
findings.fix: Decide tenancy per model explicitly. Public read models (venues/courts/availability) are NOT tenant-scoped (the marketplace queries across all venues) — gate writes by policy instead. Tenant-scope only the dashboard surface, binding the current venue from the route rather than an implicit global scope (which doesn't propagate through loaded relations, DB::table, whereHas, or raw PostGIS queries). Denormalize venue_id onto bookings for cheap scoping/constraints. Replace 'admin bypass / withoutGlobalScopes' with Gate::before super-admin policies. Document that open_matches/matches/player_ratings are intentionally platform-global. Add tests proving venue-staff tokens can't reach another venue's bookings/payments/payouts.

findings.area: Self-hosting (no-CDN) mandate vs embedded Google Map (direct contradiction)
findings.fix: 'Zero external CDN/font requests' and 'embedded Google Map' cannot both be hard requirements — an embedded Google Map loads remote JS/tiles/fonts and tanks mobile Lighthouse. Pick one and write it down: (a) self-hosted static map IMAGE on the LCP path with click-to-load facade for interactivity (recommended), (b) self-hosted MapLibre GL + self-hosted vector/PMTiles tiles for Jakarta, or (c) a documented no-CDN exception for the map only, behind a facade and excluded from the Lighthouse-gated path.

findings.area: VPR engine: Glicko-2 claimed but Elo formula given; all params unpinned
findings.fix: The update R'=R+K·g(RD)·w·(S−E) is Elo, not the Glicko-2 the spec names (no μ/φ transform, variance v, Δ, iterative σ' root-find, rating-period batching), and σ is never used — currently unimplementable. Pick ONE coherent design and write the full equations: Option A (recommended) drop the Glicko-2 label and ship a margin-aware Elo+RD hybrid (remove σ); Option B fully specify all five Glicko-2 steps (operate in μ/φ internally, map to 0–7 for display only, τ≈0.4). Pin every constant (α, K, g(RD) form, w weights, RD floor/ceiling, initial RD=max, decay rate, provisional rule) in a single config table or the §10 golden tests can't exist. Enforce idempotency via UNIQUE(match_id,user_id) on rating_history and SELECT…FOR UPDATE on the 4 rating rows ordered by user_id (concurrent workers otherwise lose updates). Keep the engine a pure function. (Defer the whole module to v1.5 per scope.)

findings.area: VPR fairness: α too steep, raw games-share wrong, doubles attribution biased
findings.fix: Three coupled fairness bugs: (1) α≈1.0 on 0–7 makes a 1.0-gap ~91% expected (real racket sport ~76%) and kills the ±0.5 matchmaking band and >2.0 damping — set α≈2.0 (1 VPR ≈ 200 Elo) with an invariant test that ±0.5 gives E∈[0.5,0.64]. (2) Raw S=games_A/total fed against a win-probability E can make a winner LOSE rating — separate outcome W from margin: S=W·(0.5+κ·(margin−0.5)) clamped [0.05,0.95], κ≈0.5, so winners never go negative and 6-0 6-0 maps to ~0.85 not 1.0; define tiebreak/super-tiebreak/min-games-threshold handling. (3) Team-average-then-flat-distribute enables the carry exploit it claims to prevent — use a stronger-player-weighted combine (e.g. 0.6/0.4) + distribute inversely to confidence + cap the weaker player's gain when the team was favoured by the partner. Add a golden test: a 2.0 repeatedly partnered with a 6.0 beating 4.0/4.0 pairs must converge BELOW 4.0.

findings.area: Open Match multi-party payment atomicity, refunds & anti-gaming
findings.fix: Model the open match as holding ONE booking on the court (same EXCLUDE constraint) for the whole window with a fill deadline ≤ hold expiry; prefer authorize-not-capture until full if the channel supports it to avoid refund churn. On deadline-not-full, auto-refund all paid participants and release (define fee bearer). Specify the open→filling→full→confirmed→completed/cancelled state machine with idempotent transitions and a concurrency guard so a 5th payer can't slip in; fee base = 1% of aggregate court fee. Add anti-collusion beyond gap-damping: confirmation quorum = ≥1 player from EACH side (not 2-of-4, staff-verify audited), decay w for repeat same-foursome matches (defuses pump rings), provisional asymmetry (can't enter a ladder/seed until RD<threshold, blocks sandbagging), and a player no-show penalty to protect the other three. (Defer to v1.5 per scope.)
